mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE growth stays below 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and business demand, which forces earnings downgrades and produces negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic below -1.5, or the interaction term loses significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth outpaces capacity constraints, signaling robust corporate output expansion that supports higher earnings and drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2% annually fails to generate positive 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observations, or the signal reverses during supply-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.5% while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, real rates turn deeply negative, prompting aggressive monetary tightening expectations that suppress equity valuations.
failure_condition: The combined threshold condition yields positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer confidence, which directly boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 150k monthly fails to predict positive 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10), or the relationship breaks down during productivity slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth combined with expanding retail sales confirms strong domestic demand momentum, which lifts corporate profit margins and drives positive equity market returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal produces negative 20-day returns in over 55% of instances, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates stagflationary supply shocks that compress profit margins and force central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months while CPIAUCSL rises
failure_condition: The stagflation trigger fails to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the signal loses predictive power when supply shocks are isolated to a single sector.
